queries.query: design the SLO program for our new payments service from scratch — help me pick the SLIs, set the targets, and draft an error-budget policy with alerting we won't get paged on for noise
we're standing up a new go service and want to get instrumentation right up front. what RED/USE metrics and span attributes should we emit so we can actually debug it later?
plan a tail-latency optimization pass for the checkout API — profile-first, with a before/after measurement gate on each change so we don't ship a regression
reduce our log bill by 40% without losing recovery value for the on-call rotation. sequence the cuts safely and tell me how to verify we didn't lose anything
design the trace context propagation and sampling topology for a new fan-out service before we write it — what should spans carry and what's the sampling strategy?
set per-operation latency budgets up-front for a new API and pick how we measure them so coordinated omission doesn't make the numbers fiction
strategize our observability roadmap for the next few quarters, anchored on the SLO program, across logs, metrics, and traces — what's the adoption sequence?
we have a program-wide cardinality problem and need an instrumentation strategy across all services — a metric-selection and cardinality budget, not a per-service fix
lift the throughput ceiling on our ingestion path — reduce queueing and coherency overhead. plan the sequence and measure before/after
we want to design the capacity envelope and saturation alerting thresholds for a new fleet before launch — what headroom policy should we set?
our checkout API p99 doubled overnight and nobody changed anything. help me diagnose what's going on
audit our observability stack across traces, logs, metrics, and slos before next quarter and score where the gaps are
on-call is being paged on noise — review our existing SLI selection and alert thresholds and tell us what's wrong
design how our cli reads config — file vs env vs flag precedence, where the config file lives per-OS, and how we validate it up front
our local build is slow — pnpm install is 45s and the jest suite is 8 minutes. design a faster inner loop for our developers
design a lower-friction 3-step consumer signup flow to cut our 60% drop-off
refactor this go handler to use early returns instead of nested if statements
